mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering sharp negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 60% or more of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power during low-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth decouples from capacity constraints, causing inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive or neutral when INDPRO rises above its 10-year trend during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent directional flip.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent, higher discount rates immediately reprice equity valuations downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in over 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that compresses wage growth expectations, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predictive regressions, or 60-day forward returns underperform the market baseline by more than 100 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which flow through to corporate revenues and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, rapid rebounds in industrial production signal restored supply chain efficiency and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% or more of post-shock recovery periods when INDPRO exceeds 95.0, or the signal fails to distinguish recovery phases from normal expansions.
